stem: The equation \( \left(2\cdot[[a]]xy+3\right)dx + \left([[a]]x^{2}+3\cdot[[b]]y^{2}\right)dy = 0 \) is exact. Find the value of the potential function \( F(x,y) = [[a]]x^{2}y+3x+[[b]]y^{3} \) at \( (x,y) = ([[x0]], [[y0]]) \), the constant defining the implicit solution curve through that point.
solution: Integrating \( M=2\cdot[[a]]xy+3 \) with respect to \( x \) gives \( F = [[a]]x^{2}y+3x+g(y) \); matching \( F_y=[[a]]x^{2}+g'(y) \) to \( N=[[a]]x^{2}+3\cdot[[b]]y^{2} \) gives \( g(y)=[[b]]y^{3} \), so \( F(x,y) = [[a]]x^{2}y+3x+[[b]]y^{3} \). At \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \), \( F = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign on the \( g(y) \) term.
This swaps the powers of \( x \) and \( y \) in the mixed term.
This evaluates \( M+N \) at the point instead of the potential function \( F \).
This uses \( y_0 \) instead of \( x_0 \) in the linear term.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.

stem: The equation \( [[a]]\sin(y)\,dx + \left([[a]]x\cos(y)+2y\right)dy = 0 \) is exact. Find the value of the potential function \( F(x,y) = [[a]]x\sin(y)+y^{2} \) at \( (x,y) = \left([[x0]], \dfrac{[[m]]\pi}{2}\right) \), the constant defining the implicit solution curve through that point.
solution: Integrating \( M=[[a]]\sin(y) \) with respect to \( x \) gives \( F=[[a]]x\sin(y)+g(y) \); matching \( F_y=[[a]]x\cos(y)+g'(y) \) to \( N=[[a]]x\cos(y)+2y \) gives \( g(y)=y^{2} \), so \( F(x,y)=[[a]]x\sin(y)+y^{2} \). At \( \left([[x0]],\dfrac{[[m]]\pi}{2}\right) \), \( F = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \cos(y) \) instead of \( \sin(y) \) in the potential.
This has the wrong sign on the \( g(y)=y^{2} \) term.
This forgets the factor of \( x_0 \) in the mixed term.
This forgets to square \( y \) in the \( g(y) \) term.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.

stem: The equation \( \left(e^{[[a]]x}y+2x\right)dx + \dfrac{1}{[[a]]}e^{[[a]]x}\,dy = 0 \) is exact. Find the value of the potential function \( F(x,y) = \dfrac{1}{[[a]]}e^{[[a]]x}y+x^{2} \) at \( (x,y) = ([[x0]], [[y0]]) \), the constant defining the implicit solution curve through that point.
solution: Integrating \( N=\dfrac{1}{[[a]]}e^{[[a]]x} \) with respect to \( y \) gives \( F=\dfrac{1}{[[a]]}e^{[[a]]x}y+h(x) \); matching \( F_x=e^{[[a]]x}y+h'(x) \) to \( M=e^{[[a]]x}y+2x \) gives \( h(x)=x^{2} \), so \( F(x,y)=\dfrac{1}{[[a]]}e^{[[a]]x}y+x^{2} \). At \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \), \( F = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the \( 1/[[a]] \) factor from integrating the exponential.
This has the wrong sign on the \( h(x)=x^{2} \) term.
This omits the rate \( [[a]] \) inside the exponent entirely.
This swaps the roles of \( x_0 \) and \( y_0 \) in the exponential term.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.

stem: For \( x>0 \), the equation \( \left(\dfrac{y}{x}+2\cdot[[a]]x\right)dx + \left(\ln x+2\cdot[[b]]y\right)dy = 0 \) is exact. Find the value of the potential function \( F(x,y) = y\ln x+[[a]]x^{2}+[[b]]y^{2} \) at \( (x,y) = ([[x0]], [[y0]]) \), the constant defining the implicit solution curve through that point.
solution: Integrating \( M=\tfrac{y}{x}+2\cdot[[a]]x \) with respect to \( x \) gives \( F=y\ln x+[[a]]x^{2}+g(y) \); matching \( F_y=\ln x+g'(y) \) to \( N=\ln x+2\cdot[[b]]y \) gives \( g(y)=[[b]]y^{2} \), so \( F(x,y)=y\ln x+[[a]]x^{2}+[[b]]y^{2} \). At \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \), \( F = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This flips the sign of the entire potential.
This swaps \( x \) and \( y \) inside and outside the logarithm.
This forgets to square \( x_0 \) and \( y_0 \) in the last two terms.
This forgets the \( y\ln x \) coefficient of \( y_0 \) entirely.
This is one more than the correct value.
This is one less than the correct value.
